newsSince: News flow is marketing/conference-led: selected as a featured exhibitor in the first Humanoid Pavilion at Automate 2026 (DEX humanoid, AI pallet-jack debut, June 24 keynote on NVIDIA-powered DEX). The fundamental backdrop is heavy dilution — a ~$38.7M January placement, ~$71.6M ATM, and a shelf for up to $200M — against tiny revenue (~$5M) and continued losses; stock -41% YTD and -11% since June 9. No transformational contract.

sources.label: Richtech capital raises / shelf (Simply Wall St)

verdicts.rankRationale: On the humanoid-stack lens, the only news is conference/marketing (Automate Humanoid Pavilion, DEX, pallet-jack) — promotional, not commercialization. Against ~$5M revenue, ongoing losses, and a $200M shelf/ATM dilution overhang, builder-purity and commercialization evidence argue for lower alpha weight vs peers.
verdicts.bull: Direct small-cap humanoid platform with NVIDIA Isaac/Jetson Thor positioning and DEX showcased at Automate 2026.
verdicts.neutral: Visible at the marquee humanoid event, but revenue is tiny and the story is still demo-stage.
verdicts.bear: Persistent losses and a $200M shelf/ATM dilution overhang; conference buzz without contract wins; -41% YTD.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Dilution from shelf/ATM; demo-stage, sub-scale revenue
